compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) is absent/bare soil. P3 (Pothos) is present with anchor. P4 (Silver Guest) is absent/bare soil.

plant_audit.p1: Empty yellow pot; no visible vegetation.
plant_audit.p2: Empty black pot; no visible vegetation.
plant_audit.p3: Pothos specimen remains; shows significant postural decline.
plant_audit.p4: No distinct specimen identified; soil appears barren.

biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P3; electronic component (IC/chip) present on desk surface in early frames, removed in later frames.

temporal_deltas.t-5_to_t-3: Minor postural shift in P3 leaf.
temporal_deltas.t-3_to_current: Progressive wilting and loss of turgor in P3; removal of external debris (electronic component).

visual_health_inference.p3_health: Critical/Declining. The Pothos leaf exhibits chlorosis and loss of structural rigidity (postural collapse).
visual_health_inference.overall_biome: High mortality rate; environment appears unsuitable for the current specimens.

anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments as a soil amendment/top-dressing.
Presence of non-botanical debris (electronic chip) on the desk surface.
Total loss of P1, P2, and P4 specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms a severe decline in the botanical collection. While the Pothos (P3) remains, it is in a state of advanced physiological stress, likely due to improper moisture management or lack of light. The other registered plants (P1, P2, P4) have suffered total systemic loss, leaving only barren soil. The environment is currently characterized by high humidity/moisture and the presence of non-organic debris, which may be contributing to the failure of the biome.
